attr-shell.comment: Trigger B: a binding whose last attrpath segment is a well-known script-carrying name, valued by an indented string. Owns the ''...'' delimiters; the inner begin/while region carries the shell so a line-spanning shell rule cannot swallow the closing ''.

shebang-shell.comment: Trigger A: inside an already-open Nix indented string, a content line beginning with #! turns the rest of the string into shell. begin/while so the region force-terminates at the '' line (the main grammar then closes the string).

shell-lines.comment: Per-line shell wrapper used inside attr-shell. Its while condition fails on the closing '' line and force-pops any shell rule the shell grammar left open across the line boundary, so control returns to attr-shell whose end consumes the ''. The begin is anchored at line-start (^) so source.shell sees ^ on the region's first content line (its ^-anchored statement rules consume the indent themselves). The (?!...'') guard mirrors the while so this rule does NOT re-enter on the terminator line (where ^ would otherwise re-match at column 0, before the closing '', and swallow it).

nix-string-escape.comment: Nix indented-string escapes: ''$ , ''' , ''\n etc. These are Nix, not shell, and must not be seen as interpolation.
nix-string-escape.name: constant.character.escape.nix

nix-interpolation.comment: Nix antiquotation ${ ... } embedded in the shell body renders as Nix, not shell.
nix-interpolation.name: meta.embedded.expression.nix
